content.text:
Yes, Office 365 will work. The current SMTP setup in `forgot-password/route.ts:40` uses nodemailer with `host`, `port: 587`, and basic `auth: { user, pass }` — which is exactly how O365 SMTP relay works.

You'd set these env vars:

```
SMTP_HOST=smtp.office365.com
SMTP_USER=your-admin@yourdomain.com
SMTP_PASS=your-password
```

**One caveat:** Microsoft deprecated basic auth for O365 in 2022 for most tenants. Whether it works depends on your setup:

| Scenario | Works? |
|---|---|
| Microsoft 365 with **SMTP AUTH** enabled per-mailbox | Yes, as-is |
| Exchange Online with **SMTP client submission** (port 587) | Yes, if the mailbox has SMTP AUTH enabled |
| Tenant with **Security Defaults** or **Conditional Access** blocking basic auth | No — you'd need OAuth2 (XOAUTH2) |

**To check:** In the M365 admin center → Users → Active users → select the mailbox → Mail → Manage email apps → confirm "Authenticated SMTP" is checked.

If basic auth is blocked, the nodemailer transport would need to be switched to OAuth2, which is more involved. Want me to add that support?

content.text:
Builds clean. Here's what to do next:

1. **Add the password** — open `.env.local` and fill in `SMTP_PASS=<your password>`

2. **Also set it in Vercel** — the env var only lives locally right now. Add it in Vercel dashboard → Project Settings → Environment Variables:
   - `SMTP_HOST` = `smtp-mail.outlook.com`
   - `SMTP_USER` = `Westcoastmarine1@outlook.com`
   - `SMTP_PASS` = `<password>`

3. **Make sure SMTP AUTH is enabled on that Outlook account** — sign in at outlook.com → Settings → Mail → Sync email → confirm POP/IMAP/SMTP is allowed. For `@outlook.com` personal accounts it's usually on by default, but worth confirming.

The full flow works like this once configured:
- User hits `/forgot-password` → enters email → gets a link to `/reset-password?token=<64-char hex>`
- Clicks link → enters new password + confirm → hits `POST /api/auth/reset-password`
- API validates the token (unused, not expired), bcrypt-hashes the new password, writes it to the DB, marks token used
- User is redirected to `/login` and can sign in with the new password immediately

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
  binaryTargets = ["native", "debian-openssl-3.0.x"]
  engineType = "client"

}

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

enum Role {
  CUSTOMER
  ADMIN
}

enum BookingType {
  LAUNCH
  RETRIEVAL
}

enum BookingStatus {
  PENDING
  COMPLETED
  CANCELLED
}

model User {
  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
  name         String
  username     String?  @unique
  email        String   @unique
  phone        String?
  address      String?
  passwordHash String
  role           Role      @default(CUSTOMER)
  suspendedUntil DateTime?
  createdAt      DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt      DateTime  @updatedAt

  vessels              Vessel[]
  dailyMessagesUpdated DailyMessage[] @relation("DailyMessageUpdatedBy")
  manualBookings       Booking[]      @relation("ManualBookingCreatedBy")
  passwordResets       PasswordReset[]
}

model Vessel {
  id         String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId     String
  boatName   String
  boatType   String?
  boatNumber String
  yardNumber String?  @unique
  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt  DateTime @updatedAt

  owner    User      @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  bookings Booking[]

  @@index([userId])
  @@index([yardNumber])
}

model Booking {
  id              String        @id @default(cuid())
  vesselId        String
  type            BookingType
  serviceDate     DateTime
  requestedAt     DateTime      @default(now())
  status          BookingStatus @default(PENDING)
  completedAt     DateTime?
  stayingOnMooring Boolean      @default(false)
  note            String?
  createdByUserId String?

  vessel      Vessel @relation(fields: [vesselId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  createdBy   User?  @relation("ManualBookingCreatedBy", fields: [createdByUserId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)

  @@index([type, serviceDate, status, requestedAt])
  @@index([vesselId, type, serviceDate])
}

model PasswordReset {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String
  token     String   @unique
  method    String   // "email" | "phone"
  used      Boolean  @default(false)
  expiresAt DateTime
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([token])
  @@index([userId])
}

model DailyMessage {
  id               String   @id @default(cuid())
  serviceDate      DateTime @unique
  message          String
  launchStartTime  String?
  retrievalStartTime String?
  updatedAt        DateTime @updatedAt
  updatedByUserId  String?

  updatedBy User? @relation("DailyMessageUpdatedBy", fields: [updatedByUserId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)

  @@index([serviceDate])
}

Deployed successfully. Live at **https://westcoast-marine-parks.vercel.app**

Along the way, this also fixed several underlying issues that were lurking:
- Prisma 7 schema migration (`url` moved to `prisma.config.ts`)
- `nodemailer` and `twilio` added to `package.json` dependencies
- `@types/nodemailer` added
- `prisma/seed.ts` excluded from Next.js TypeScript compilation
- `@types/pg` version conflict resolved with a cast in `db.ts`
